examples.input: A class of 50 students went boating at a park. The rental information is as follows: a large boat can seat at most 6 people and costs $10 to rent; a small boat can seat at most 4 people and costs $8 to rent. They rented a total of 10 boats, and each boat was filled to capacity. How many large boats did they rent?

A ship sails between two ports, A and B. The journey downstream takes 5 hours, and the journey upstream takes 7 hours. If the speed of the current is 5 kilometers per hour, what is the distance between port A and port B?
When Leo asked his teacher, Mr. Smith, for his age, Mr. Smith replied, "When I was your age, you were 3 years old. By the time you reach my current age, I will be 45." How old is Mr. Smith now?

A worker planned to make a batch of parts within a scheduled time. If he makes 10 parts per hour, he would produce 3 more parts than required in the allotted time. If he makes 11 parts per hour, he would finish 1 hour ahead of schedule. How many parts are in the batch?

Two stations, A and B, are on a railway line 408 kilometers apart. A slow train departs from station A at a speed of 72 kilometers per hour, and a fast train departs from station B at a speed of 96 kilometers per hour. If the two trains travel in opposite directions, away from each other, after how many hours will they be 660 kilometers apart?

A shop sells a tourist souvenir. In April, the total revenue was $20,000. To increase sales volume, the shop offered a 10% discount on the souvenir in May. As a result, the number of items sold increased by 200, and the revenue increased by $7,000. What was the selling price of the souvenir in April?

A department store finds that it sells an average of 20 items of children's clothing per day, with a profit of $40 on each item. For every $1 the price is reduced per item, the average daily sales increase by 2 items. If the store wants to achieve a daily profit of $1200, what is the maximum amount by which the price of each item should be reduced?

To complete a certain task, Alex would take 20 days working alone, and Ben would take 10 days. The task must be finished in exactly 12 days. If Ben works for a certain period and then Alex takes over to complete the remaining work, for how many days must Ben work?

A can complete a project alone in 10 days, and B can complete it alone in 15 days. If A works alone for 5 days first, and then A and B work together until 2/3 of the entire project is completed, how many days did they work in total?
The marked price of a certain shirt is $100. If it is sold at 80% of the marked price, a profit of 25% is still made. What is the cost price of each shirt?
The price of 1 cup and 1 kettle is $45, and the price of 3 cups and 2 kettles is $99. What is the price of 1 cup?

A class received a batch of tickets to distribute to all students for volunteer sales. If each student receives 9 tickets, there are 24 tickets left over; if each student receives 10 tickets, there is a shortage of 16 tickets. How many students are there in the class?

During a holiday discount for a certain clothing brand, Mike bought a set of clothes for a total of $400. It is known that the coat was sold at 80% of its original price and the pants were sold at 70% of their original price. If the combined original price of the coat and pants was $540, what was the original price of the coat?

Ethan and Mike set off simultaneously from school to the Science Museum to participate in an activity. Ethan walked at a speed of 6 km/h, and Mike walked at a speed of 8 km/h. After walking for 1 hour, Mike realized he had forgotten his materials and returned to the school to retrieve them, then immediately chased after Ethan along the original route. How many hours after leaving the school again did Mike catch up with Ethan?

A clothing wholesale store sells a certain brand of shirts, originally making a profit of $30 per shirt. With the arrival of the off-season, the store prepares to sell at a reduced price. A survey found that if the price of each shirt is reduced by $x, then (20+x) shirts can be sold per day. If the daily profit is $616, how much should the price of each shirt be reduced in order to clear the inventory as quickly as possible?

A development company produced a certain number of new products that require precision processing before being released to the market. Factories A and B both want to process this batch of products. It is known that Factory A and Factory B can process 16 and 24 units of this product per day, respectively. Additionally, it is known that if working alone, Factory A takes 20 days longer to process this batch than Factory B. What is the total number of units of this new product?

A certain telecom operator offers two types of mobile phone cards. The charging standard for Card A is as follows: no monthly fee, and a charge of $0.6 per minute of talk time. The charging standard for Card B is as follows: a fixed monthly fee of $40, plus an additional charge of $0.4 per minute of talk time. For how much talk time will the charges for Card A and Card B be the same?
David bought 2 pairs of shoes at 80% of the original price, saving $20 compared to the non-discounted price. How much did he actually spend on these 2 pairs of shoes?

Stations A and B are 480 km apart. A slow train departs from Station A at 90 km/h, and a fast train departs from Station B at 140 km/h. The two trains start simultaneously and travel in the same direction, with the slow train behind the fast train. After how many hours will the distance between the fast train and the slow train be 600 km?
The grandfather's current age is 5 times that of his grandson. In 12 years, the grandfather's age will be 3 times that of his grandson. How old is the grandson now?
A high school is organizing a basketball tournament for 9th-grade students. Each pair of classes plays one match against each other. If 15 matches are planned, how many classes are participating?

To control the spread of influenza, the health department studied a certain type of flu and found that if 1 person becomes infected, there will be a total of 100 infected people after 2 rounds of transmission. If the transmission rate remains the same, what will be the total number of infected people after the third round of transmission?

The athletes at a certain school are being grouped for training. If each group consists of 7 people, 5 people are left over; if each group consists of 8 people, there is a shortage of 3 people. What is the total number of athletes in the school?

A department store sells brand-name shirts. It usually sells 20 shirts per day with a profit of $40 per shirt. To expand sales, increase profits, and minimize inventory as much as possible, the store decided to adopt appropriate price reduction measures. A survey found that for every $4 reduction in the price of a shirt, the store can sell an average of 8 additional shirts per day. If the store wants to make an average daily profit of $1200, what is the maximum amount by which the price of each shirt should be reduced?

Sarah, a fruit shop owner, purchased a quantity of fruit at $4 per pound and sold it at $6 per pound, selling 150 pounds per day. A survey found that for every $0.1 reduction in the selling price per pound, an additional 30 pounds could be sold per day. To ensure that at least 360 pounds are sold daily, Sarah decided to reduce the price. In order to make a daily profit of $450, by how much should Sarah reduce the selling price per pound?

Several people are planting trees together. If each person plants 12 trees, 6 saplings are left over; if each person plants 15 trees, there is a shortage of 6 saplings. Find the number of people participating in the tree planting.
The cost price of a coat is $360, and the marked price is $600. To achieve a profit margin of 0.25 on cost, at what percentage of the marked price should the coat be sold?

To organize a set of books, it takes 60 hours for one person working alone. A group of people worked for 1 hour first. Then, 15 more people joined them, and they worked together for another 2 hours, exactly completing the task. Assuming everyone works at the same rate, how many people were in the initial group?

A company produces a certain product with a cost of $400 per unit and a selling price of $505. To further expand the market, the company decides to reduce the selling price per unit by 4% while simultaneously reducing the cost, which results in a 5% increase in sales volume. By how much should the cost per unit be reduced to ensure that the company's total sales profit remains unchanged before and after the price reduction?

The FIFA World Cup qualifiers follow a home-and-away round-robin format, meaning that each pair of teams plays two matches, one at each team's home ground. If a total of 110 matches are held, how many teams participated?

With a wind speed of 25 km/h, it takes an airplane 2.8 hours to fly from Airport A to Airport B with the wind, and 3 hours to fly the same route against the wind. What is the distance between Airport A and Airport B in kilometers?
In a shopping mall, a jacket was priced at 50% above its cost price. Later, for a promotion, it was sold at 80% of the marked price for $240. What is the cost price of each jacket?

A certain type of clothing had an original price of $150 per piece. After two consecutive price reductions, the current selling price is $96 per piece. Find the average percentage of each price reduction.
One person is infected with the flu. After 2 rounds of transmission, a total of 100 people are infected. On average, how many people does one person infect in each round?

At the start of the new school year, a parent asked a teacher how many students were currently in the class. The teacher replied: "If another group of students equal to the current number joined, plus half of the current number, plus a quarter of the current number, and if your child also joined the class, there would be exactly 100 people." Please help this parent figure out how many students are currently in the class.

An orchard originally planned to plant 100 peach trees, with each tree yielding an average of 1000 peaches. Now, it is planned to plant more trees to increase the total yield. Experiments found that for every additional tree planted, the yield of each tree decreases by 2 peaches, but the number of additional trees cannot exceed 100. If the total yield is to increase by 15.2%, how many additional peach trees should be planted?

Leo went to a stationery store to buy pencils. The shop owner said, "If you buy more, you can get a 20% discount." Leo calculated that if he bought 50 pencils, it would be $6 cheaper than the original price. What is the original price of each pencil?

A store is preparing to sell a certain type of clothing at a discount for a seasonal clearance. If each garment is sold at 50% of the marked price, there will be a loss of $20. However, if it is sold at 80% of the marked price, there will be a profit of $40. What is the marked price of each garment?

A store purchased a batch of clothing at a cost price of $50 per piece. If sold at $60 per piece, 800 pieces can be sold. For every $1 increase in the selling price per piece, the sales volume decreases by 20 pieces. If the goal is to make a profit of $12000 while ensuring the total cost of the items sold does not exceed $24000, what should the selling price of the clothing be?

A store sells a product with a cost price of $40 per kilogram. If it is sold at $50 per kilogram, 500 kilograms can be sold in a month. For every $1 increase in the selling price, the monthly sales volume decreases by 10 kilograms. For this product, the store wants to achieve a monthly profit of $8000 while ensuring the total sales revenue does not exceed $20000. What should the selling price per kilogram be?

A store adjusted the price of a certain product, selling it at 80% of its original price. At this price, the profit margin of the product is 10%. The cost price of the product is $160. What is the original price of the product?

To attract tourists, Summit Travel Agency introduced the following fee structure: if the number of people does not exceed 25, the per-person travel fee is $1000; if the number of people exceeds 25, for every additional person, the per-person travel fee decreases by $20, but the per-person travel fee cannot be lower than $700. A company organized its employees for a trip and paid a total of $27,000 to the travel agency. How many employees in total went on this trip?

John and Tom are practicing for a race. John runs at 7 meters per second, and Tom runs at 6.5 meters per second. If John gives Tom a 5-meter head start, in how many seconds will John catch up with Tom?

A class of students went boating. If 7 students sit in each boat, then 5 students are left without a seat; if 8 students sit in each boat, then there are 2 empty seats. How many students in this class participated in the boating trip?
When the students of a class graduate, each person must take a 2-person photo with every other student in the class. If a total of 780 photos were taken, how many students are in this class?
Several workers are working at a brick factory. If each person carries 2 bricks, then 6 bricks remain; if each person carries 4 bricks, all bricks are moved. How many workers are there?

On this New Year's Day, Sarah spent $88 to buy two types of gifts, A and B. Type A gifts cost $12 each, and Type B gifts cost $8 each. She bought 1 fewer Type A gift than Type B gifts. How many Type A gifts did she buy?

A shopping mall sells a new type of desk lamp with a cost price of $300 per unit. Market research indicates that when the selling price is set at $400 per unit, the average monthly sales volume is 300 units. For every $10 increase in the selling price, the average monthly sales volume decreases by 10 units. At what selling price will the average monthly profit for this type of desk lamp reach $40,000?
The marked price of a piece of clothing is $300. If it is sold at 80% of its marked price, a profit of $60 will be earned. What is the cost price of this piece of clothing?

It is known that 4 empty mineral water bottles can be exchanged for 1 bottle of mineral water. If there are currently 15 empty mineral water bottles, what is the maximum number of bottles of mineral water that can be drunk without spending additional money?

A pile of peaches is to be distributed among a group of children. If each child is given 5 peaches, 8 peaches will remain; if each child is given 8 peaches, they will be 7 peaches short. How many peaches are in this pile?

A shopping mall sells a batch of shirts. On average, it sells 20 shirts per day, with a profit of $45 per shirt. Affected by the international financial crisis, the mall decided to implement appropriate price reduction measures to reduce inventory as quickly as possible. Market research shows that for every $1 price reduction per shirt, an average of 4 more shirts can be sold per day. If the mall wants to achieve an average daily profit of $2,100, what is the maximum price reduction for each shirt?

The cost price of a certain product is $100, and the marked price is $150. Due to overstocking, the store plans to sell it at a discount. To ensure a profit margin of at least 20%, what is the maximum discount they can offer?

A project takes Jack 10 days to complete alone and Bill 15 days to complete alone. If Jack works on it alone for 5 days, and then Jack and Bill work together to finish the remaining part, how many more days will it take?

The marked price of a product on the 4th floor of Central Mall is $1540. If it is sold at a 10% discount, it still yields a profit of 10% relative to the cost price. What is the cost price of this product?

Michael rides his motorcycle at a constant speed from his home to the train station. If he travels at 50 km/h, he will arrive 15 minutes later than the train's departure time; if he travels at 60 km/h, he will arrive 5 minutes later than the train's departure time. What is the distance from Michael's home to the train station in kilometers?

The travel information for a scenic area is as follows: if the number of people does not exceed 30, the cost per person is $80; if the number of people exceeds 3, for every additional person, the cost per person decreases by $1, but the cost per person cannot be lower than $55. A company organized a group of employees for a trip to this scenic area and paid a total of $29,25 in travel fees. How many employees participated in this trip?
The marked price of a certain product is $200 per unit. When it is sold at a 10% discount, a profit of $20 per unit is still earned. What is the cost price per unit of this product?

To encourage residential electricity consumption, a city's power company has established the following billing method: for monthly usage not exceeding 100 kWh, the rate is $0.50 per kWh; for monthly usage exceeding 100 kWh, the excess portion is billed at $0.40 per kWh. If a certain household's average cost per kWh in February 2002 was $0.48, how many kWh of electricity did the household use in February?

Grandpa is about to celebrate his 80th birthday. Jerry wants to circle this day on the calendar, but he doesn't know which day it is. When he asks his dad, his dad smiles and says, "On the calendar, the sum of the four dates above, below, to the left, and to the right of that specific day is exactly equal to Grandpa's age." What is the date of Grandpa's birthday?

In the 2018-2019 Chinese Basketball Association (CBA) season, a double round-robin system was used (where every two teams play each other twice). If the total number of games played was 380, how many teams participated in the competition?

Team A originally had 65 workers, and Team B originally had 40 workers. Now, 30 additional workers are being assigned to these teams. In order for the number of workers in Team B to be 1/2 of the number of workers in Team A, how many workers should be assigned to Team A?

A workshop received a processing task and planned to process 120 pieces per day to finish on schedule. In actual production, they processed 20 more pieces per day than planned, which resulted in the task being completed 4 days ahead of schedule. How many pieces were there in total for this processing task?

To expand sales, a store adjusted the selling price of a certain camera, selling it at 20% off the original price. At this time, the profit margin is 14%. If the cost price of this camera is $1200, what was the original selling price of the camera?

A children's clothing department found that an average of 20 pieces can be sold daily, with a profit of $40 per piece. To increase sales volume, the store decided to implement a price reduction. Market research indicates that for every $4 price reduction per piece, an average of 8 more pieces can be sold per day. To achieve an average daily profit of $1200 from this item, what is the maximum price reduction for each piece of clothing?
Mary got married at the age of 26 and gave birth to a son the following year. Several years later, Mary's age is 3 times her son's age. How old is Mary at this time?
The marked price of a certain product is $800. Even if it is sold at a 60% off, it still yields a profit of $60. What is the cost price of this product?

Three construction teams worked together to build a highway. The first team built 1/3 of the total length of the road, the second team built 1/3 of the remainder, and the third team completed the highway by building the final 20 kilometers. How many kilometers long is this highway in total?
A product of a certain brand is sold at a 20% discount off its marked price, and it still yields a profit of 10%. If the marked price of this product is $275, what is its cost price?

In March, the total workload of the 4 workers in Group A exceeded 4 times the monthly per-person quota by 20 pieces. In contrast, the total workload of the 5 workers in Group B was 20 pieces short of 6 times the monthly per-person quota. If both groups completed the same actual average workload per person, what was the monthly per-person quota?

With New Year's Day approaching, various shopping malls have designed promotional strategies to boost consumption and profits. Downtown Mall marked up the price of a certain schoolbag by 50% above its cost price and then offered a 20% discount. By doing so, the mall earns a profit of $8 on each schoolbag sold. What is the cost price of this schoolbag?

A fast food restaurant is trial-selling a certain combo meal. After a trial period, it was found that the cost of each meal is $5, and the restaurant's fixed daily operating expenses are $600 (excluding meal costs). If the selling price per meal does not exceed $10, 400 meals can be sold daily; if the selling price exceeds $10, for every $1 increase in price, the daily sales volume decreases by 40 meals. If the restaurant's daily net income is $1560, what should the selling price per meal be, given that the restaurant aims to offer the most favorable price to its customers?

The clothing department of a shopping mall sells a brand-name shirt at an average of 30 units per day with a profit of $40 per unit. To expand sales and reduce inventory, the mall decided to implement a price reduction. Market research shows that for every $1 decrease in the price per unit, an average of 2 more units can be sold per day. If the mall requires the department to achieve a daily profit of $1200, by how much should the price of each shirt be reduced?

A class of students was divided into two groups to participate in an activity. Group A had 26 students and Group B had 32 students. Later, due to the needs of the activity, some students were transferred from Group A to Group B. As a result, the number of students in Group B was 1 more than twice the number of students in Group A. How many students were transferred from Group A to Group B?
A factory plans to increase the annual output of a certain product from the current 1 million units to 1.21 million units over the course of 2 years. What is the average annual growth rate?

A cycling team is training, with all members moving forward at a speed of 35 km/h. Suddenly, Rider No. 1 speeds up to 45 km/h and travels for 10 km alone, then turns around and rides back, still at a speed of 45 km/h, until rejoining the team. How many hours elapsed from the moment Rider No. 1 left the team until they reunited with the other members?

To promote table tennis, a school purchased some sports equipment. The total cost of the nets, paddles, and ping-pong balls was $1320. The costs of these three items were in a ratio of 3:6:2. How much money was spent on the nets?

Manager Wilson brought back several bags of local specialties from a business trip to share with his friends. If he gives 5 bags to each friend, 3 bags remain; if he gives 6 bags to each friend, he is 3 bags short. How many bags of local specialties did Manager Wilson bring back?

A school bought some exercise books to distribute to the seventh-grade students. If each student is given 5 books, 80 books will remain; if each student is given 6 books, they will be 40 books short. How many seventh-grade students are there?

It takes Arthur 2 hours to drive from Point A to Point B, and it takes Bob 3 hours to ride his motorcycle from Point B to Point A. If Bob sets off from Point B toward Point A, and 1 hour later Arthur sets off from Point A toward Point B, how many hours after Arthur departs will they meet?

After inquiring at two stores, the prices were found to be $25 per set of paddles and $2 per ball. Store A offers a 10% discount on both paddles and balls. Store B offers a promotion where purchasing one set of paddles comes with two free balls. If a customer needs to buy 2 sets of paddles and a certain number of balls, how many balls would make the total cost equal at both stores?

The distance between Station A and Station B is 448 km. A slow train departs from Station A traveling at 60 km/h, and a fast train departs from Station B traveling at 80 km/h. If both trains depart at the same time and travel toward each other, how many hours after the fast train departs will the two trains meet?

A family of three plans to travel to a northern destination during the holidays. There are two travel agencies, Summit Travel and Horizon Travel, which have the same base pricing but offer different family discounts. Summit Travel charges full price for adults and offers a 40% discount for children. Horizon Travel offers a 20% discount for the entire family. Upon calculation, Horizon Travel is $240 cheaper than Summit Travel. What is the base price per adult?
The cost price of a certain type of flower is $5 per stem. If it is sold at a 20% discount off the marked price, it still yields a profit of $3 per stem. What is the marked price per stem?

A highway is being built in a certain area. If Team A works on the project alone, it takes 80 days to complete; if Team B works on the project alone, it takes 120 days to complete. Team A and Team B are now contracted to work together. How many days will it take to complete the project?

A biology laboratory needs to culture beneficial bacteria. Starting with 60 living samples, the total number of bacteria reaches 24,000 after 2 rounds of cultivation. In each round, through division, each bacterium generates a certain number of new bacteria in addition to the original one. On average, how many new bacteria does each bacterium produce in each round?

A craft club of 30 students is making paper airplane models. Each student can make either 20 fuselages or 60 wings per hour. One airplane model requires 1 fuselage and 2 wings. To ensure that the parts produced each hour match perfectly, how many students should be assigned to make the fuselages?

A set of books is being distributed to the students of a class. If each student is given 3 books, 20 books will remain; if each student is given 4 books, there will be a shortage of 25 books. How many students are in this class?
A certain product was marked up by 20% over its cost price to set the marked price. It was then sold at a 10% discount for $270. What is the cost price of this product?

In a class, one person catches the flu. After two rounds of transmission, the total number of infected people reaches 49. Based on this same infection rate, if 4 people initially have the flu, what would be the total number of infected people after the first round of transmission?

To green the campus, the students of a certain class planted a total of 144 tree seedlings. Each boy planted 3 seedlings, and each girl planted 2 seedlings. If there were 8 more boys than girls, how many boys were in the class?

Before the holiday season, Daily Mart sold a batch of products with a cost price of $12 each at a selling price of $20 each, selling 240 units per day. After some time, it was discovered that for every $1 increase in price, daily sales would decrease by 20 units; however, for every $1 decrease in price, daily sales would increase by 40 units. If the store chooses to reduce the price, by how much should each unit be reduced to achieve a daily profit of $1800?

At a trade fair, every two participating companies signed exactly one contract with each other. If a total of 78 contracts were signed by the end of the fair, how many companies attended this trade fair?

Warehouse A currently stores 37 tons of grain, and Warehouse B stores 17 tons. If an additional 15 tons of grain are to be distributed between the two warehouses, how many tons should be allocated to Warehouse A so that the total amount of grain in Warehouse A becomes twice the amount in Warehouse B?

A sports equipment store sells a certain brand of bicycles. It is known that 1 skilled worker and 1 new worker can assemble a total of 8 bicycles per day, while 3 skilled workers and 5 new workers can assemble a total of 28 bicycles per day. How many bicycles can 1 new worker assemble per day?
Chickens and rabbits have a total of 192 legs. If the number of chickens and the number of rabbits are swapped, there would be a total of 198 legs. How many chickens and how many rabbits are there?
There are 18 more chickens than rabbits, and together they have a total of 84 legs. How many chickens and how many rabbits are there?
